listings.dealer: Jeff D'Ambrosio Chrysler Dodge Jeep Mits

truecar.note: TrueCar body-style filter still broken — same finding as previous run. No convertibles surfaced in budget.

top10.introHtml:
Refreshed Mon May 11, 03:22 PM ET. Pulled live convertible inventory from cars.com, CarGurus, AutoTrader, and TrueCar inside a 35-mile radius of 19063 (Media, PA), $7,000–$15,000 range, no accidents / clean title. Below: top 10 hand-ranked for a cheap-fun-second-car brief — Miata-friendly, soft-top reliability over miles, mechanical simplicity over badge.

TrueCar's body-style filter wasn't honored — see source notes. Cars.com / CarGurus / AutoTrader returned full convertible inventory.

picks.whyGood: The Mazda MX-5 Miata is the gold standard for cheap second-car convertibles — light, simple, mechanically bulletproof, and cheap to insure. NC-generation (3rd gen) at well under 100k miles, the sweet spot for parts availability and remaining life. Soft top is simple, manually-operated and inexpensive to replace.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the top through a full open/close cycle and inspect rear-window seam (window is glass on NC). Look for clutch slip on test drive. Check for oil leaks at the rear main seal. Confirm no rust on rocker panels.
picks.bottomLine: Bullseye for this brief — inexpensive, fun, almost impossibly reliable for the money.

picks.whyGood: Pontiac Solstice on the GM Kappa platform with a 'Great Price' rating from AutoTrader and remarkably low miles for the year. Naturally-aspirated 2.4 Ecotec is durable. Well-flagged price suggests room to negotiate.
picks.whatToVerify: Top operation is the #1 thing — Solstice tops are heavy and the latches stick. Check trunk space when top is down (almost none). Inspect convertible top seals for leaks at A-pillar. Confirm recall work on TPMS.
picks.bottomLine: Best dollar-per-smile pick under $10k in this batch.

picks.whyGood: Saturn Sky is the platform-twin of the Solstice with arguably better styling and only 30k miles on the clock — exceptionally low for an 18-year-old roadster. Same simple naturally-aspirated 2.4L drivetrain.
picks.whatToVerify: Verify the low miles with a Carfax/AutoCheck — listings this low warrant scrutiny. Same convertible-top checks as the Solstice. Sky-specific trim pieces are getting hard to find.
picks.bottomLine: If the miles check out, this is a museum-grade Sky at a market price.

picks.whyGood: Z3 2.8 with the inline-six — more grunt than the 1.9 four, less complex than the M coupe motor. 75k miles is low for the year. Closest pick to Media on the list.
picks.whatToVerify: Inspect the rear subframe mounts for cracks (a Z3-specific issue). Operate the soft top fully — listen for hydraulic motor noise. Check cooling system (plastic radiator and expansion tank fail with age). Confirm transmission in person.
picks.bottomLine: Private-seller risk, but if the car checks out and it's the manual, it's the deal of the bunch.

picks.whyGood: Time-capsule Solstice at 32k miles. Pricier than pick #2 but with half the miles on the chassis and from a dealer that lists prices clean.
picks.whatToVerify: Same top + A-pillar seal checks as picks #2 and #3. With this few miles, look hard at tire age (10+ year-old original tires happen) and brake fluid color.
picks.bottomLine: Premium price for premium preservation — second choice if the cheaper Solstice is gone.

picks.whyGood: The 128i is the naturally-aspirated 3.0L — no turbo plumbing, no HPFP-failure stories. E88 convertibles are the modern E36/E46 replacement at half the price. Close to home.
picks.whatToVerify: Oil leaks at the valve cover and oil filter housing gaskets are nearly guaranteed at this age — budget $400 for service. Check the soft-top hydraulic pump operation. Verify cooling system has been done in past 30k.
picks.bottomLine: BMW soft-top fun without the turbo headaches — well-priced for the year and miles.

picks.whyGood: Final-year E88 128i — 'Great Deal' rating with sub-70k miles. Same N52 naturally-aspirated six as pick #6 but newer body and lower miles for only $1,500 more.
picks.whatToVerify: Same valve-cover/filter-housing leak inspection as the 2010. Run the top through 3-4 cycles to confirm hydraulic pump health. Look for water staining on the headliner above the C-pillars.
picks.bottomLine: If the inspection comes back clean, this is the sharpest BMW value of the lot.

picks.whyGood: Last year of the SN-95 Mustang GT — 4.6L V8, simple drivetrain, parts cheap and everywhere. Sub-67k miles is genuinely low for the year. Strong dealer rating reduces purchase risk.
picks.whatToVerify: Listen for the 8.8 rear-axle clunk on takeoff. Check for top-frame rust (SN-95 frames can corrode). Verify which transmission — manual T3650 had third-gear synchro issues; auto 4R70W is bulletproof. Inspect convertible top motor in trunk.
picks.bottomLine: Old-school V8 cruiser with cheap maintenance — best 'simple American' pick.

picks.whyGood: Dealer-backed Z3 2.3 (170hp inline-six) at the same mileage as pick #4 but with a real lot to inspect at and warranty options. A safer route to a Z3 if private-seller risk is a no-go.
picks.whatToVerify: Same Z3 checks as pick #4 — rear subframe cracks, top operation, cooling-system age. Selden Motors rating is mediocre (3.3) — get an independent PPI.
picks.bottomLine: Backup Z3 with dealer support if the cheaper private-seller pick falls through.

picks.whyGood: Newest car on the list — F57 generation MINI with the BMW B48 turbo four. Power top, modern infotainment, easier to live with day-to-day. Recent price drop suggests negotiation room.
picks.whatToVerify: B48 timing-chain tensioner service history (early B48s ate them). Power-top motor and roof seals — these tops can leak around the front header. Check run-flat tires for wear. Confirm transmission type.
picks.bottomLine: Modern, comfortable, slightly more risk on long-term cost — pick this if you want one car you can drive every day.

mustangs.introHtml: Ford Mustang convertibles only. Ranked V8 GT first, manual preferred, low miles preferred, then closer-to-home. 3 currently listed in the $7k–$15k range within 35 mi.

picks.whyGood: Mustang GT — 4.6L V8 (1996+) or 5.0L (1994–95), the iconic powertrain. Manual transmission — the right gearbox for a fun second car. Low miles (55,000) for a 1995 Mustang.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the convertible top through a full open/close cycle — listen for hydraulic motor strain. Check the rear axle for clunk on takeoff (8.8 rear is robust but bushings wear). SN-95 chassis (1994–2004): inspect for rust where convertible top frame meets rocker panels. T-3650 manual: 3rd-gear synchro can be weak — slow-shift into 3rd to test. Look for water staining on rear seats / carpet — top seal failure is common.
picks.bottomLine: V8 + manual + drop-top — the textbook fun-second-car build.

picks.whyGood: Mustang GT — 4.6L V8 (1996+) or 5.0L (1994–95), the iconic powertrain. Low miles (44,000) for a 1994 Mustang.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the convertible top through a full open/close cycle — listen for hydraulic motor strain. Check the rear axle for clunk on takeoff (8.8 rear is robust but bushings wear). SN-95 chassis (1994–2004): inspect for rust where convertible top frame meets rocker panels. Look for water staining on rear seats / carpet — top seal failure is common.
picks.bottomLine: V8 grunt with the easy-driving auto — solid daily-fun balance.

picks.whyGood: Mustang GT — 4.6L V8 (1996+) or 5.0L (1994–95), the iconic powertrain. Low miles (66,885) for a 2004 Mustang.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the convertible top through a full open/close cycle — listen for hydraulic motor strain. Check the rear axle for clunk on takeoff (8.8 rear is robust but bushings wear). SN-95 chassis (1994–2004): inspect for rust where convertible top frame meets rocker panels. Look for water staining on rear seats / carpet — top seal failure is common.
picks.bottomLine: V8 grunt with the easy-driving auto — solid daily-fun balance.
